_meta.purpose: Seed directory of .town profiles for dottown.ch / Mightville. Every .town name gets a profile — available ones are for sale, taken ones still get a page (the directory is the network effect, not just the inventory).

statusKey.taken: registered by someone else — profiled anyway, as concept

_meta.note: All profile copy is original. 'alignment' ties each town to the Dayville/Nightville duality.

towns.ethos: The industrious town. Consensus is forged here, not declared — heated, hammered, and tested before it holds.
towns.knownFor: Never missing a vote. The strongest treasury locks in the federation.

towns.ethos: The open-treasury town. Wealth held in common and in full view — every coin accounted for where any child can read it.
towns.knownFor: The most transparent ledger of all the towns. Flagship of the movement.

towns.ethos: The connective town. Conducts trade, messages, and trust between all the others — the federation runs through here.
towns.knownFor: The routes. If two towns deal, the wire passes through Copper.

towns.ethos: The town of preservation and memory. Coastal, patient. Nothing true is ever thrown away.
towns.knownFor: The archive — the deepest record in the federation. Salt remembers.

towns.tagline: The last coal lights the next morning.
towns.ethos: The resilience town. Burned down once and chose to stay. Small fire that outlasts any blaze.
towns.knownFor: Coming back. When a thing collapses anywhere, Ember teaches it how to relight.

towns.ethos: The contemplative town, high in the clear air. Slow, careful law made by people who think before they freeze a decision in.
towns.knownFor: Clarity. Other towns send their hardest questions up the mountain.

towns.ethos: The experimental town. Embraces upheaval as method — rapid governance trials, fast iteration, nothing sacred but the willingness to change.
towns.knownFor: Trying everything first so the cautious towns can copy what survives.

towns.ethos: The refuge town. Takes in the lost and the arriving. You land a stranger; you leave a citizen.
towns.knownFor: Welcome. The first stop for anyone who left a country behind.

towns.tagline: Shade for those not yet born.
towns.ethos: The green, patient town. Plants for the next generation. Growth measured in decades, not quarters.
towns.knownFor: The forest it tends. Every law must answer: is this good in fifty years?

towns.tagline: Gold counts the day; silver keeps the night.
towns.ethos: The moonlit counterpart to Gold — the night-treasury, where what's valued isn't only what's counted.
towns.knownFor: Being claimed already. (Profiled anyway — the good names go fast.)

towns.tagline: We carry the word, and never lie in it.
towns.ethos: The messenger town — communication and the trust graph made flesh.
towns.knownFor: Already registered. Some birds were always going to be claimed.

towns.tagline: Some names are claimed by their meaning.
towns.ethos: Sanctuary. Of course it's taken — a word like that doesn't sit on the shelf.
towns.knownFor: Being gone before you got here.

towns.ethos: The proof of concept: the best names are gone, which is exactly why a directory of ALL of them — owned or not — is the real asset.
towns.knownFor: Reminding us that inventory runs out, but the map of towns never does.
